article_title: Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee Meeting — July 16, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to hear update on PRC facilities project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a verbal update on the PRC Facilities Project covering four sites: Shadbolt main stage theatre and studios, Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre, Cameron Community Centre and Library, and Brentwood Community Centre. Photos from June 2026 were presented showing construction progress at each location. No changes to timelines, budgets, or scope were announced.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch the council meeting or review the staff presentation to stay informed about project timelines and potential impacts on local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
VERBAL REPORT: PRC FACILITIES PROJECT UPDATE
1.
PRC Facilities Project Update - 2026.07.16.pdf
Staff presentation by Mary Chow, Director Civic Projects; Jackie Flowers, Chief Librarian; Emmaline Hill, Director Cultural Services;  and Isabelle Taillon, Recreation Expansion and Activation Lead
agenda_briefs.article_title: Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee Meeting Agenda — July 16, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to simplify permits and safety rules for community event producers
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a staff report on improvements for community event producers, including a new centralized web page (Burnaby.ca/BurnabyEvents), a simplified online intake form, and enhanced safety planning resources. New resources include guidance on parades, hostile vehicle mitigation, crowd management, and weather-related planning, plus a simplified Health and Safety Plan template and Risk Assessment Tool. These changes are already implemented for the 2026 event season.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Use the new online intake form at Burnaby.ca/BurnabyEvents as your first point of contact when planning a community event.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY EVENT PRODUCERS
1.
Support for Community Event Producers - 2026.07.16_.pdf
Purpose: To provide an overview of improvements to support community event producers focused on simplifying access to City services, strengthening event safety planning, and enhancing the overall customer experience.
Staff presentation by Emmaline Hill, Director Cultural Services and Lois Dawson, Manager Special Events

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Learn to Swim pilot program update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive an update on the Learn to Swim Pilot Program, which has provided eight 30-minute swimming lessons over four weeks to 248 Grade 5 students from three Burnaby schools since Winter 2025. The program uses $206,000 in one-time funding approved for 2025 and $412,000 for 2026. Sessions are held at Fred Randall Pool between 12:00 p.m. and 2:00 p.m. due to staffing and scheduling constraints. Five schools are projected to participate in Fall 2026. The program aims to expand based on school interest and operational capacity.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Scheduling changes at Kensington Outdoor Pool in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposal to change the scheduling at Kensington Outdoor Pool, following a referral from the June 9, 2026, CCP committee meeting. The proposal, based on correspondence from Alex Goldkind, requests reinstating dedicated outdoor lane swimming periods, preserving fitness-oriented swim opportunities for adults and seniors, scheduling large school groups (100–200 students) outside peak public swim times, and consulting regular users before future schedule changes. A final decision has not yet been made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the upcoming council vote on this scheduling change — it could affect your operations or programming.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Daycare access, parking changes at Christine Sinclair Centre
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal that could change how daycare access, parking, and FIFA-related impacts are managed at the Christine Sinclair Community Centre. The proposal, referred from a June 9, 2026 committee meeting, is still under consideration and has not been approved yet. If passed, it could affect how local businesses and families use the centre's parking and daycare services, especially during large events like FIFA matches. A resident reports approximately 10 parking stalls are currently marked with orange cones as 24-hour reserved, and requests 2–3 short-term daycare pickup/drop-off spaces near the entrance.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your business's parking access or daycare operations near the centre.
agenda_briefs.industries: Childcare & Daycare, Parking & Transportation, Event Management
